channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: FIRE is mental illness — 173 replies — 692.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
FIRE is mental illness

Open thread • Replies: 173 • Updated: 2026-05-22 18:04 UTC

OP

No woman, no kids, $25k YEARLY spend.
You're not supposed to check out of society in your 30s and spend the rest of your life relaxing. What is wrong with modern men?

Early replies (first 3)

62259938
>>62259935
Keep slaving away goyman

62259945
>>62259935
Nice bait thread faggot

62259968
>>62259935
You can't really afford to marry on FIRE because one divorce ruins a decade+ of saving and then you have to work again. If a woman finds out you're FIRE she will absolutely divorce rape you too since the wealth disparity between you and her is so great she'll walk away with a ludicrous amount of money.

Huge downside of FIRE, you can never marry.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262335
>>62259993
>>62259984
>>62259980
I'm not googling your gay term.
God bots fucking suck.

62262345
>>62260055
> 60k is pretty good money in flyover states
For starting out or early in your career, yeah it’s good. If you’re older than 28-29 I would say it’s not.
>t flyover

62262353
>>62260080
>something something definition of insanity
If someone gets divorced twice and remarry again clearly they’ve got a few screws loose

62262359
>>62262353
i liked that character

62262485
>>62259935
>NO GOY YOU ARE DOING IT WRONG YOU ARE SUPPOSED TO CHASE HOLLOW MATERIAL POSESSIONS, TRAP YOURSELF IN PERMANENT SLAVERY AND PRODUCE FUTURE SLAVES!!1!

How about you go and zuck yourself, Schlomo?

62262639
>>62260231
> The problem is in modern dating its very difficult to pass that first barrier
That’s my struggle. Not going to say I’m some chad, but I’m successful in many aspects. I’m successful with women once I get my foot in the door, but like you said, it’s incredibly difficult in today’s world.

62262648
>>62262485
kek

62262721
>>62259997
Because once you get beyond the "haha I'm traveling bit" it's kinda a shitty place to live. If you're going to be a monk and have a shit apartment and spend your day playing vidya and eating goyslop with your cat, but Panama also involves banging hookers, I guess.

62262729
>>62259980
> net worth will greatly exceed your peers and especially any women in your age bracket.

Yes it's much better to be a poorfag to get married, lmao. How fucking stupid are you? And why the fuck would you marry a working woman who is going to keep working anyways? Who is taking care of your house and homeschooling your kids and baking cookies for the church function?

62262791
>>62261282
I mean, men getting married and reproducing is obviously important on paper. I see why society needs it. But this society doesn’t deserve it. They’ve shit on men for too long. I refuse to even consider marriage until circumcision is outlawed in the USA.

Which will never happen, but that’s fine by me.

62262812
>>62261290
>Notice how I said I’d kms
They won’t let you, retard. You’ll get Baker Acted, and they’ll stuff you into a fun little room with no bed sheets or sharp edges. And you’ll sit there until you give up the gold. The cameras will “malfunction,” and the guards will step into your room and beat the piss out of you. You think you’re the first genius to think of this bullshit?

62262837
>>62261427
>100 blow jobs in 2 years is actually some kind of record. Even the best blow job queen i ever met preferred to have her pussy fucked than to blow me.
I’m counting all the times she sucked my dick before I put it in. Only counting the times she just fully sucked me off, it wasn’t that many.

Anyways, marriage is a joke. God designed women to not enjoy men that much as another middle finger to men. Everything sucks.

62262844
>>62261285
You esl ? How is having a kid virtue signaling again? Why demoralize people

62262879
>>62262314
>Prenups can be pretty ironclad even in gay states
Lol, so many retards on this board just fucking blabbering about shit they know nothing about. In some states, the judge can just throw out the Pre-Nup for no reason at all. The judge just has to want to throw it all because she feels like it. If you get a female judge in a state like that, you’re fucking done for.

You fucking teenagers don’t know shit about divorce court in the USA. You genuinely don’t know fucking shit. You know that infidelity doesn’t mean shit for alimony in most states. Your wife can suck 10,000 dicks after your wedding day, and you have to pay her to suck those dicks. And you pay her until one of you dies or she gets remarried. Which she’ll never get fucking remarried cuz the gravy train stops. I have an uncle that was married for 4 years, and he’s been paying his ex-wife alimony for 30 years. You don’t know shit.

62262891
>>62262721
>Because once you get beyond the "haha I'm traveling bit" it's kinda a shitty place to live
This is literally the USA. I’ve lived outside the USA for months at a time, and I’ve never missed fucking Chipotle or 10,000 McDonald’s in every zip code.

62262925
>>62262891
>picrel
She's way overqualified, and she looks like that? You're telling me no HR department would hire her? Has she even tried?

62262942
>>62259935
If he really wants to keep being FIRE I would probably move to latin america or asia and have a kid with a girl there. Cheaper COL and if they separate it probably won't hurt him as much financially.

62262946
>>62261134
Are you uncircumcised? I heard a lot of girls don't like sucking uncut penises because of the smell. Not that there's anything you can really do about it but thats the reason why

62262961
>>62259935
found the kike

62262966
>>62260605
You get a 1.6% return? You’re lying or completely retarded. I’m assuming alarm by a retard.

If you really had 2.5M you could lock up over 5% in 30yr treasuries right now. Thats $125k a year guaranteed.

62262975
>>62260877
> Cash out you retard before the market crashes and you lose your 1.3m.
Still waiting on this crash I’ve been hearing about for over a decade

62262982
>>62261016
Ok. So what? You sell, split the gain, and you keep all your income generating assets which have also likely appreciated in value. Or she keeps the house and buys you out. Or you keep the house and buy her out.

It sucks, but it’s a hardly “lose everything” situation.

62263085
>>62259935
It's not that great, I had 120 million a one point i spent a few years living in a covered lead zinc mine done as as a kind of evil villian lair (firing rage, cinema, arcade, even has a garden with a little watterfall) alone aside from very rare meetings. I came back up to find out I've kind of disconnected from everyone except god. I spend most of my time doing direct charity work (it's no good giving it to charities it winds up with some cunt paying himself a salary). everyone thinks I'm homeless or something when they meet me.

The only advise I have is get a dog. I've gone though every form or flavor of woman possible and yes, I have kids who are provided for and are now in their mid 20s and I spent a two decades looking after. never hear from them because they hsve no idea how wealhy I am (one told me that I should not be eating cake in a hotel if I was uneployed!). One used to surface to meet at Christmas and it's birthday to get a haul of presents but did no even bother to call his year.

Such is life. I'm geting tired of life on he surface again, maybe another year and I am going back underground.

62263105
>>62263085
me

62263162
>>62261104
Why would she record that? It’s not like she’d know you were going to say it. Not like you’d repeat it either.

62263168
>>62261112
I think that’s why he said fuck you I’m playing another year and winning another super bowl

62263178
>>62262879
This my first wife ran off with someone off the internet when my kids were toddlers and took three houses and cleared me out. She married me probebly because i had a couple of million at that point and as soon as the kids were in school and creche she screwed me over. She has no idea how wealthy I became either alhough I made sure the kids got everything they needed and wanted and good educations, university paid all that. She sold the three houses and the kids cut contact with her completely and like I say were on touch with me and I used bring them on holidays and that for about three months a year. I heard she was actually working at a mcdonalds drive in at one point.

Its funny how many friends and people who love you appear when they think ou are rick and fascinating how they vanish when they think you don't have money. a bit grim but true.

Funny but true, I own a shed load of banksy I got when they were 100$ just because I liked them and one kids was going on and on about art to me, so I said here and gave hem a copy of last exist from the giftshop. Thining if they showed any interest at all, I'd give them a million worth. The kid told me that they did no hink much of it and had no inerest in it. I'd bet if I had showed hem where i is stored and how much it was all worth they would have been interested.

I'll tell you people are what they are. nothing more. I loved my father and used call him ever day for a chat and made sure mother was comfy.

If your woife divorces you, she ges pre much everhing ou have at the time of divorce and you pay child support until the kids are ou of university but it does not go to them it goes to he mother, you will have o pay for everything from sneakers to schoolbags to clubs and university fees and healthcare and xboxes because ou can be absolutely certain she won't. For mos men no faul of heir own i is basicall impossible. i know i was very very lucky. Still a poinless exercise for me at least

62263190
Anyway buy no I only come back to post onn this board ever few years and I posted yesterday and today. That's enough,.See you all in another few years.

62263239
Oh well. Just be rich and buy sex.
Retired at 27.

62263275
>>62261451
correct minus the desire to live in the woods. would prefer to live by the water. I like being innawoods walking/hiking sometimes backpacking, just no desire to live there permanently.

item.title: PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS. — 76 replies — 304.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
PACK. A. SANDWHICH. WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-05-22 18:12 UTC

OP

PACK. A. SANDWHICH.

WITH. SOME. CHIPS.

Early replies (first 3)

62262487
>>62262449
maybe don't buy guac if you are poor and it costs $4.90

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262904
>>62262449
Of course someone as fat as that piece of shit couldnt afford to eat out. Normal people eat once a day. My net worth would be 1% higher over my entire life if I cooked at home.

62262906
>>62262769
Youre fuckin weird.

62262916
>>62262549
B O O T S T R A P S

62262987
>>62262872
Maybe half that

62262992
>>62262449
Caleb is a zillennial and doesn't remember when shitty fast food was $5

62263030
>>62262906
You're a faggot kikeslave

62263066
>>62262454
This is just engagement bait. Ramsey may or may not genuinely be an ignorant boomer but it’s irrelevant because he knows his brand image is that of the ignorant boomer. So that’s exactly what he gives his audience.

62263087
>>62262454
this 70 iq boomer is a billionaire and u dont even have 10M

how does this make you feel?

62263110
>>62262474
people in china eat out 3 times a day btw they dont even cook.

62263113
>>62263087
And hes 65 and going to keel over in the next 10 years. Enjoy that billionaire status bucko! Hope it was worth spending his time making fun of Zoomies on a podcast instead of traveling or enjoying life

62263122
>>62263113
That’s a lot of seethe, keep being poor

62263127
>>62262649
yes the asians have it completely right.. all you need is some rice or noodles, veggies and meat tossed in some sauces

you literally dont need more eat that twice a day every day and its perfect

62263131
>>62262817
>$4/hr
>Minimum wage workers hoarding gold

62263146
>>62263131
$4 an hour back then is $20 an hour today but taxes and costs were a lot lower too

62263155
>>62263127
Asia proved exactly why you need meat and enough calories anon. Indian vegetarians are retarded. Japanese are considered smart, and guess what, they were short as fuck and retarded a couple generations ago. Once they started eating meat they grew massively. Koreans are the tallest and they eat meat. The less meat you eat, the lower IQ, and the lower your height.

62263160
>>62263146
And imagine starting a business in 1970. You just need a shovel and truck. Now you need endless fucking paper work and certifications and fee's.

62263163
Yeah just eat chips and Onions and bread every day so your thin and weak and then get blamed for being thin and weak.

The humiliation rituals just never end do they.

62263177
Why do people ignore the fact that buying expensive lunches is only one symptom of their useless spending? They act like they dont spend more money on other useless shit and subscriptions.

62263197
>>62262449
I have a business trip to one of the mega expensive Nordic countries and I literally might bring bread and peanut butter and jam in my checked bag because fuck paying $50 for a burger and fries.

62263202
Eating doordash is literally rat poison quintupled

62263206
>>62263177
The gym is another one. I’m sick of how so many go to the gym. They think of it as a healthy choice. It wastes all their time and money on an activity that doesn’t matter at all. You can get 95% of the same benefits with a dip and pull up bar. Fast. Do some pull ups and dips. You’ll be built like a prisoner. Instead they spend like $300 a month on membership, clothes, food, supplements. It’s a fucking psyop.

62263217
>>62262526
$10k a year in investible post tax money is huge. That could decrease your retirement age by half a decade. That is also around 25 days per year of extra work days that you didnt have to work if you were making $50 an hour.

62263218
>>62263206
Is anon admitting that the poor and incarcerated are just as skilled as him? Sounds like a good reason to stop paying him

62263225
>>62262740
I'll eat whatever, but Starkist Tuna on rice is just Eew.

62263229
>>62262906
it's not working little yidcel

62263252
>>62263218
I don't understand either sentence.

62263257
>>62263206
Holy shit I want to squat and bench and walk uphill on a treadmill and sit in the sauna (yes I ice my balls). My gym is literally $11.99 a month and I'm not a poorfag.

>$300 a month on membership, clothes, food, supplements

If you're in an area that's not white then you need to spend a premium to get away from the dangerous minorities.

Getting high protein foods that are calorie efficient involves something of a premium, if you also want that to be super easy and shelf-stable then you really need to spend more.

You don't need to spend crazy amounts of money to get relatively jacked but you do need more than a pullup bar and dips.

62263268
>>62263252
You clearly long for the prisoner life, you aspire to be like them.

62263272
>>62262449
>>62262474
Thats a lot of sugars. Swap the apples out for a raw veggie and the PB&J out for ham or turkey w/ cheese. Will cost almost exactly the same. Point stands though if you arent retarded and have even caveman level cookies abilities grocery costs are almost a non issue until you have kids. All my coworkers live at home and eat out 3 meals a day and wonder why they have money problems. I have my own place and easily afford it because im not stupid and am capable of cooking, and dont finance $40,000 sports cars.

62263303
>>62263268
Low IQ faggot.

item.title: THE DECOUPLING HAS ARRIVED — 26 replies — 104.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
THE DECOUPLING HAS ARRIVED

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-22 18:09 UTC

OP

The mad lad really did it bros, check the charts

Early replies (first 3)

62261996
OH FUCK IM GONNA DECOUPLEEEEE

62262000
>>62261990
>>62261996

KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!!!!!!!!!!

SO FXCKING BULLISH CHAINLINK SAAAAAAAAAAAAR

62262002
Kabooooom

Links going to $20 by end of month!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262010
>>62261990
Bros…what if this is it? If the singularity happens are you ready?

62262013
it's beginning

62262023
This rally will fail like all the others.

62262030
>>62262010
Well I sold 1k to realize some profits for the probable reckoning coming in days to week to months at the latest, but still have 4k stinks. Hopefully can turn into somethin nice. Little upset realized some of my lowest cost basis lots but I wanted to take some profits. My main concern is moving to a state without carry forward capital loss balance year over year, otherwise I’d continue realizing the bad remaining lot buys ($15/$16 range)and have a nice bank to offset gains down the road

62262046
>>62261990
Top is in, for crypto in general.

62262054
>already dropping
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62262062
>>62262013
YEH SERGEY! FUCK IM UP!

62262285
Do you guys think the run to 1k is going to be a scenario where you wake up and link is up 300% and everyone is losing their minds, or do you think it's going to be a 10 year run of +2% days and never having a red day again?

62262300
time to crash btc i guess

62262350
>what's that? link at $10!
>pull it.

62262393
>LINK hits 10.03
>BTC takes a dive

Im just not gonna sell

62262397
ok boomers

62262405
>>62262285
after all the fake and gay market manipulation bullshit, it deserves to go straight to 1k in a single candle

62262418
>>62262405
This is exactly how XRP holders think

62262427
>>62262418
>don't believe your lying eyes goy!
bitcoin is used to manipulate the price of link, I don't give a fuck about your whataboutisms

62262533
>>62262427
Its funny how by doing that shit they erased 99% of people from buying Link but the 1% that bought have metric fuckloads and a vengeance. Almost worse.

62263186
>>62262418
XRP isn't getting adopted by DTCC later this year.

62263224
>>62262285
Link is never going close to 40 ever again kek and the only way it makes a new ath, is BTC going upwards of 500k. Very unlikely scenario for the next 15 years

62263238
>>62263224
>the only way it makes a new ath, is BTC going upwards of 500k
Nah the only way Link makes a new ath is BTC not dumping every time Link moves.

62263256
>>62263238
Bro cmon, be honest, we're at literally at 0.00012 sats, we got raped by Sirgay and it's over. We have lost, it's just another shitcoin and it will never recover in any meaningful way, while king shitcoin has a chance of making new aths.

62263278
>>62263256
>we got raped by Sirgay
Fucking how?

We got raped by 6 years of Bitcoin dumping on every single Link pump and announcement.

62263287
>>62263278
Are you retarded?
I literally bagholded link from .00169 sats to the shit it is now at 0.00012, i would had made it having sold all my Link and converted to BTC, now I'm a bagholder and definitely selling all my unstaked shit if it reaches 20-25

62263293
>>62263287
Yes, because we got raped by 6 years of Bitcoin dumping on every single Link pump and announcement.

item.title: How much can you invest into tax free portfolios in your country? — 16 replies — 64.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How much can you invest into tax free portfolios in your country?

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-22 18:11 UTC

OP

How much can you invest into tax free portfolios in your country? (not including pensions)
>UK
20k per year

Early replies (first 3)

62261497
>>62261457
$7,500 in Roth IRAs
$24,500 in Roth 401k
>USA

There are others but I'm not looking it up.

62261502
>>62261457
bold of you to assume that the uk wont simply change the rules once enough brown communists are in parliament

62261505
>>62261457
goymany
0

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261507
>>62261457
All portfolios are tax-free in my country, lol

62261513
>>62261457
>UK
>20k per year
Plus £9,000 in Junior ISA's.

62261530
>>62261497
>$24,500 in Roth 401k
I said not including pensions

62261532
>>62261507
Which country?

62261534
>>62261530
401ks aren't pensions. They're fundamentally different.

62262204
>>62261534
Ok, I should have said not including retirement plans

62262371
>>62261457
in Brazil the government sometimes would let you invest in fund with some of your social security money... in a fund that holds 1 stock and has 2% management fee

it's all so tiresome

62262392
>>62262371
What's the stock?

62263042
>>62261532
Luxembourg, no tax after 6 months of holding

62263260
>>62263042
noice. isnt it boring there tho

62263276
>>62261457
0, in 2028 I'm gonna get raped with 36% unrealized gains on all investments with the almighty generous allowance of 1.8k euros tax free per year if I have gains, kek.
Nobody cares, just bring more infinite niggers since I'm leaving this shithole.

62263279
>>62262392
Macaco

62263298
>>62263276
You’re Dutch, aren’t you?

item.title: If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal o… — 8 replies — 14.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal o…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-22 17:39 UTC

OP

If you were sub 120 IQ and had to start over career wise RIGHT NOW, what career field would you get into with the goal of maximizing against AI replacement, that ISN'T a trade?

Early replies (first 3)

62260419
>>62260402
>that ISN'T a trade?
kek'd

62260433
>>62260402
Health care seems pretty safe in my opinion. People still need to be seen in person by nurses or get scanned/tested by techs for a myriad of endless health issues in our increasingly unhealthy population.
One huge problem though is nearly every single medical facility in America, even health core courses, mandate the covid vax because we're all mindraped goykike'd cattle slaves.

62260473
>>62260433
Health Care is a trade in reality.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62260481
>>62260433
It seems like a good option, I don't know if I could handle being around "customers" all day, but thought about doing xray tech

62260486
>>62260402
Seriously become a tradie and build your local data center.

62260523
>>62260402
Production, shipping, transportation, storage, delivery… of physical resources that are vital to the AI economy (water, energy, rare earth elements, silicon, gallium, germanium, helium, palladium, tantalum, copper, bromine, lithium, etc.).

AI needs all that, in gigantic quantities.

If you are in America, this is considered a major strategic issue for the government and the largest companies out there, so you could even get great funding if you can provide these resources to the major AI players in America.

Don’t fight AI, fight for AI.

62260526
>>62260486
How?

62263144
>>62260402
Sugging diggs

item.title: Guys we've been trading down consolidating sideways for 4 years now, this isn't funny anymore. — 5 replies — 13.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
Guys we've been trading down consolidating sideways for 4 years now, this isn't funny anymore.

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-22 17:50 UTC

OP

Guys we've been trading down consolidating sideways for 4 years now, this isn't funny anymore. Since the Terra Luna Do Kwon rug, the market has never truly recovered. FTX Sam Bankman Fried dealt the killing blow, and despite BTC reaching a new all time high when orange man was sworn into office, that money was never reallocated to alts.

Real World Assets coming on chain may be our only hope, what other narrative is there? AI? lol

Early replies (first 3)

62262930
>>62262909
retard. you are wasting your money and time. people realized what altcoins are, a quick pump and dump scheme. they are never going to come back. in the meanwhile stuff like spy is outperforming bitcoin let alone all the altcoins. what the fuck are you hoping for? wake the fuck up.

62262938
Just trade stocks. Crypto isn't coming back until the market isn't giving 8 baggers.

62262951
Memes, tokenized stocks, and privacy coins. What more do you want?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262973
>>62262951
Think about it, the crypto companies and their "altscoins" were always treated as stocks by traders. The top crypto companies generated barely any revenue, idk why people are shocked that most alts went to zero.

62263204
>>62262909
>FTX Sam Bankman Fried dealt the killing blow
in the sense that SBF regularly invested in altcoins and the destruction of his exchange destroyed the biggest altcoin liquidity provider, yes

item.title: 16 years ago today Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for 2 pizzas — 16 replies — 10.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
16 years ago today Laszlo Hanyecz paid 10,000 BTC for 2 pizzas

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:45 UTC

OP

Two pizzas aren’t on the table; they’re sitting at it. This was a deliberate act of rub, clearly signalling to the right crowd. BTW, where are these kids now? Because I can find his recent photos, but zero photos of *his* kids.

Early replies (first 3)

62261169
>>62261166
Still more valuable than anything else bitcoin has ever done

62261183
Why isn’t this diverse

62261194
>>62261183
ew

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261207
>>62261166
haha yeah wouldn't that be funny. lucky me even back then it was impossibly hard to mine so i have none

62261263
>>62261166
bro those kids are 20+ years old they don't live at home anymore dumb fuck, at that age parents get a picture or two a year if lucky. less if they live out of town.

62261274
>>62261263
That dad is 100% gay you can see it on his face and his faggy kids, similar to that jewish actor Sabretooth whos son was sucking on his bike handle like it was a sucker, now he's a famous trans femboy.

62261301
>>62261166
>2010: 10,000 BTC can buy two pizzas
>2026: 10,000 BTC can buy two nationwide pizza chains

62261391
>>62261166
Looks like what I ordered on a date last week, I said to her "What are you ordering?" LOL

62261396
>>62261166
>10,000 bitcoin for two pizza's
>creepy pic with two kids as evidence
Yeah, sure, and John Podesta has a diverse collection of handkerchiefs, righto

62261400
>>62261166
Bitcoin wouldn't be where its out without that purchase

62261517
>>62261263
>bro those kids are 20+ years old they don't live at home anymore dumb fuck
Alright, where is his wife then? There's zero photos of him and mother of *his* kids

62261678
>>62261391
Nice one Saul

62261685
>>62261166
child trafficking right there in front of everyone all in the name of monetary freedom

62261687
>>62261517
Not everyone broadcasts their life's online you zoomer fuck. There are literally zero images of me on the internet outside of some old Facebook friends from 10 years ago and I have been online since 95

62262003
10,000 BTC is currently worth $771,584,132 USD
Ouch. So you're telling me he spent nearly a billion on two crappy pizzas? I hope he kept some and didn't blow his full stack on this purchase. Does anyone know if he's worth millions today?

62262910
>>62261274
>>62261166
why are you such a massive faggots? you are stinky ALTers, aren't you.
fuck off, losers

item.title: American Reserve Modernization Act of 2026 — 5 replies — 4.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
American Reserve Modernization Act of 2026

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-05-22 17:08 UTC

OP

The US Bitcoin Strategic Reserve will be the new Fort Knox.
ARMA is being drafted, and a major announcement will soon ignite the Bitcoin race.
I hope you're not waiting to buy until October.

Early replies (first 3)

62260596
>>62260467
that would be quite the exit scam

62260630
>>62260596
>200,000 btc bought each year for 5 years
>20 year holding requirement
That's an exit scam how?

62261654
The American Reserve Modernization Act (ARMA) would authorize the U.S. Treasury to acquire up to 200,000 bitcoin per year for five years, targeting a reserve of 1 million BTC.

The bill was introduced Wednesday by Rep. Nick Begich with 16 original cosponsors. It is a rebranding and expansion of the original BITCOIN Act co-introduced last year by Begich and Senator Cynthia Lummis. It would codify Trump's March 2025 executive order establishing a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve into permanent law that cannot be reversed by a future president.

The bill classifies bitcoin as a "Tier 1" strategic reserve asset, putting it on the same legal footing as gold. A separate digital asset stockpile would hold other federally owned crypto.

All acquisitions must be budget-neutral. The funding mechanism is revaluing Federal Reserve gold certificates from their current statutory price of $42.22 per ounce, set in 1973, to current market prices. That gap generates hundreds of billions in accounting gains without new taxpayer debt.

The bill also ends the practice of auctioning off seized bitcoin. All future seizures would be transferred directly to the Strategic Reserve instead of being liquidated by the U.S. Marshals Service. Existing government holdings would be consolidated into a single audited ledger.

Bitcoin in the reserve would be held for a minimum of 20 years. The bill establishes federal custody standards including geographic distribution of private keys across air-gapped facilities, multi-signature governance requiring authorization from the Treasury, the Fed, and an independent third agency, and investment in "quantum-resistant cryptographic upgrades."

Rep. Pat Harrigan, one of the cosponsors, said "The United States government already holds billions in seized bitcoin with no coherent strategy for managing it, and that needs to change."

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261807
>>62261654
I don't understand why people are bearish. Do they have an intellectual disability?

62263017
>>62260467
A nice idea, but I doubt it'll pass. Republicans are atrociously bad at passing legislation

item.title: NTDOY — 55 replies — 58.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
NTDOY

Open thread • Replies: 55 • Updated: 2026-05-22 17:16 UTC

OP

Currently Nintendo stock is extremely mispriced. All the short-term doom and gloom is priced in, ignoring the company's tremendous long-term upside. While it's not some 10x your money in a week meme coin, at this point it's a sleeper stock to hold you should know about.

Early replies (first 3)

62259388
>>62259371
its not mispriced. they make shitty games for 80+$. no one wants to buy their crap because its shit.

62259405
>NTDOY baggie
KWAB

62259407
>>62259371
not misspriced
no point in shilling your bags here btw
>>62259388
this and console era is coming to an end. Future will be only game stations with steam like platforms. retards still go for the ps5 by some sort of pavlovian reflex. it's just a standardized computer with a standardized controller. Nintendo shined for a while 30 years ago now it has become so cringe i can't stand their adds like wtf is even this shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62259808
>>62259746
Mid 1980s: people predict video game market dead due to major crash and low-quality games
Late 1980s: people predict video game market dead due to arcade cooldown and home console "fad"
Mid 1990s: people predict video game market dead due to "CD Rom bubble panic" as commercial failures like Atari Jaguar and 3DO make people think industry is imploding
Early 2000s: people predict video game market dead due to 3D games becoming the norm and production budgets exploding
Mid 2000s: people predict video game market dead due to popularity of browser and web-based alternatives
Early 2010s: people predict video game market dead due to widespread popularity of mobile and social media gaming alternatives
Mid/Late 2010s: people predict video game market dead due to massive DLC, microtransactions, and "loot box" gambling dynamics
Early 2020s: people predict video game market dead due to "battle royale bubble" with high concentration in games like Fortnite
2026: random faggot on /biz/ predicts video game market dead because "this time it's different"

62259814
>>62259371
Switch 2 owner here. The new console sold very well due to the positive sentiment created by the Switch 1, and the hardware is certainly most pleasant to hold and see. Other than that, it’s a total disappointment.

>no games, the current lineup is both paltry and boring as fuck
>battery is kill after 2 hours
>storage on the console is rather low
>almost every 3rd party game is a fucking "game key card" that combines the worst of physical (having to carry a cartridge) and the worst of digital (takes the little storage you have)
>hardware self-destructs even if you take great care of it, my left joycon stick looks like it took 15 years of beatings after just a few months (it never happened with anything else I own)

Because of these problems, I’m buying 10 times fewer games on the Switch 2 compared to the Switch 1, and I will not buy a Switch 3 in the future given the more than mediocre treatment we get here.

TL;DR Nintendo gives the illusion of doing well with their numbers, but they’re actually shitting the bed. Disappointing performance will ensue if you are a long term shareholder.

62259828
>>62259814
The console is still in its infancy. Every criticism you have is fixable. New console-seller titles are inevitable. Imagine the Switch 2 Pro with battery and storage upgrades, etc.

62259884
>>62259808
Video games aren't dead. Consoles and reliance on proprietary IPs is. Gaming continues in phones for casuals and PC indies for the hardcore crowd. It's funny because had you invested in phones and social media instead of gaming companies since the early 2010s you'd be massively better off than an investment in Nintendo. Who's talking about "it'll be different this time"?

And anyway, yes, the world is different in 2026 than it was in 1980. Investment is about the future, not staring at previous success and insisting it'll happen again. What is Nintendo doing that could possibly replicate their boom in the 1980s?

62259900
>>62259828
It’s fixable, but will it be fixed? So far, nothing indicates that Nintendo takes the problem seriously, especially with the "no games" problem. Looks like Nintendo will just release four or five decent AAA games through the entire console’s lifespan (one Mario, one Zelda or whatever, and these are not even announced) and then call it a day.

62259983
>>62259371
Why would people be hungry for buying an ever increasing in price console (due to RAM) with zero new Zelda or Mario on it?

62260004
>>62259814
Dude, SW2 is a stop gap filler console until the real one (with a new hardware gimmick) wil be ready, the big games (mainline mario/zelda) will come out very late to be also used as launch titles for the next.
This is wii u/gba/3ds all over again.

62260032
>>62259371
>making this thread again
Please god nuke the third world

62260035
>>62259371
I agree. Not because of vidya, but because of Pokémon cards. The market cap of all PSA10 graded Pokémon cards is over $75B. Nintendo's market cap os only $53B. It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.

62260053
>>62260004
Nintendo could actually get away with it (releasing a Switch 3, 4, 5, etc. forever) by not being so retarded and underwhelming… Instead they will be forced to invent something else that may or may not be successful.

62260188
>>62259371
Thanks

62260304
>>62259371
agreed p/e is slurpable, nintendo aint going away

62260335
>>62259884
already priced in

62260390
Apple is apple

62260407
I bought a Rog ALLY X and it’s 100000x better than the switch I had for my son. You know the target audience? My kid uses the Ally to watch YouTube play actual fun games. He cares more about Spider-Man than bing bing wahoo slop. Nintendo failed to capture the audience by marketing to childless manchildren just like Disney Adults. They have no market share and sell an inferior product. Pokémon still looks and plays like an indie game from 2005. (lol)

Enjoy your bags tendie you get what you deserve.

62260606
>>62260407

62261314
Sorry about your hopes of pumping your bags OP, but I'll just leave this here...
>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=luiKV1nHB1w

62261355
>>62259371
The switch 2 is overpriced. People who have money don't even know what it is. It also demotes the value of the switch one, which partially went subscription based model. So it was bad business design.

62261416
Tell me the last time they ever release a ground breaking software or hardware? The new pokemon is not a cashgrab.

62261518
anyway, you know when a retard here shill some stock:
1/ he is clueless no amount of shiling here will pump it not even 0.01%

2/therefore it's a bad pick

it's cringe.last retard shilled mc donald like it was some secret gem. The real blackpill is whatever gets shilled here don't buy it or even better sell it if you have some (pokemon cards, random crypto that miraculously pumped x10, nintengoy stocks, mc goynald stock, whatever)

62261624
>>62260035
>It makes no sense that the company that makes the cards is worth so much less than the cards themselves.
What psychogenic kush are you smoking? It makes a ton of sense. By your logic, a goldsmith can't have created a masterpiece work that he has sold 20 years ago but which is now recognized to be more valuably than his entire shop.

62261625
Value Trap 2: Electric Boogaloo

62261640
>>62259371
I agree. They are the one last big truly JP dev remaining, huge franchise value that's only getting better, the only one console with exclusives, ninety-something % of the serious handheld business with proper customer attachment, JP approach to quality as opposed to deadlines, no DEI, success with theme park now expanding, huge success with the films now.
No reason not to own their stock, I think it's a good 50% undervalued.

62261642

>Nintendo has suffered a drawdown of 50%
>ZOMG invest in Nintendo!!

62262381

62262518
>>62262296

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62262525
>>62262381

62262540

Lol. Lmao.

62262541
>>62262518

apparently only the DUMPs are manipulation
All pumps are natural and blessed by the anal juice from the All Mighty himself

62262568
>>62262541
lastly, look at plebbbit
Try posting a link to an article about the war ending and you'll probably get banned
Oil bulls are insufferable

62262656
>>62262540
>ideologically subverted golem argues with strawman in his head

62262703
>>62262308
>>62262381
Oil is already almost double the price it was before. How delulu are people who think it should be at 150-200? Thats literally impossible with the state of the global economy. A recession would drag it dowen immediately. I said since months 100 is the fair price for a close straight and it remains true.

62262713
>>62262525
>reality of supply and demand
Supply AND demand. Demand dropped massively, especially is asia. Its also beeing substituted. Supply didnt even decrease much, Saudi Arabia just sells via pipelines now. Usa massively increased production. Russia massively increased export.

62262725
>>62262308
The oil FUD causes there to be less effort put into conservation of oil and also less effort put into increasing production of oil

62262741
>>62262713
Demand dropped by 2.5 million barrels, supply by 7 million barrels.

62262747
>>62262713
>Saudi Arabia just sells via pipelines now.
The Saudi pipeline is capped at 7mbpd. The UAE pipeline is capped at 1.8mbpd.

> Usa massively increased production
This is not true. US crude production has remained flat. In fact, the US is currently producing slightly less crude than it did earlier this year
https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCRFPUS2&f=W

>Demand dropped massively
Well obviously; demand must match supply (counting stockpiling and stock drawndowns), so if there is less supply, then demand destruction has to occur somewhere

62262755
>>62262745
>>62262747
the usa doesn't want oil to go up, case closed! Buy AI stocks and semi-conductors if you want an easy time making money. These are the things the USA wants to go up.

62262805
>>62262713
>Russia massively increased export.
I don't think this is true either. Russia's export of crude oil has remained approximately flat. Russia's export of refined oil product has declined somewhat due to disruptions to refinery operations caused by drone attacks.

https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/russian-seaborne-oil-product-exports-fell-april-amid-drone-attacks-data-industry-2026-05-15/

62262822
>>62262296
Nothing wrong with a small position in oil stocks but being a straight up oil baggie and essentially betting against the global economy is straight up retardation

62263048
>>62262805
Ask AI about russian seabourne oil sanction waiver
Bessent just renewed it. renews it 17th of each month

62263058
BTW the same people calling oil to $200 are the same people who jumped on the silver to $200 and GME.

62263069
>>62263048
Didn't largely effect volumes of export.

62263089
>>62262525
>the reality of supply and demand

item.title: Anon, its my bday today Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in th… — 27 replies — 19.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, its my bday today Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in th…

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-22 16:51 UTC

OP

Anon, its my bday today
Im in early 30s, close to no friends, no gf (ditched a bitch around 2 years ago), not much in the bank, had a successful career but then pivoted to make a startup and lost shit ton of money and time in the process. Not sure what to do anymore. Basically going through the dark night of the soul.

Now I'm here trying to find a tech job in a fucking US to not die on the street. You are, as usual, my only real friend.

Tell me something nice or not.

Early replies (first 3)

62261420
>>62261414
>Now I'm here trying to find a tech job in a fucking US
Are you a foreigner? Is this a LARP?

62261422
You did good

62261428
>>62261414
Your life can turn around in a week easy. New job, new girl, market pumps, whatever. Don't be so down anon you don't know what is around the corner. Also you're only early 30s, you're young as fuck, you have to live as long as you already have all over again before you hit mid 60s, just think how long that truly is. Stop worrying and enjoy your life you fag.

Happy Birthday, enjoy the magic trick.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62261442
>>62261428
whoops wrong file

62261445
Doing way better than I am

62261472
>>62261420
yes im from EU

62261490
>>62261428
thanks anon, i will try my best, there is nothing to do for me anyway

62261501
>>62261414
the answer lies at the end of a shotgun barrel.

You know this, i know this, we all know this yet we cling to life like ticks

62261504
>>62261472
Go back to your country

62261512
>>62261504
I really don’t understand why an euro would want to move to USA these days. T. An euro

62261523
>>62261512
not being paid 20 euros per hour max?

62261539
>>62261512
We have much lower barriers to starting a business or making money.

other than that, yeah. US isn't having a great time right now.

62261551
>>62261539
>We have much lower barriers to starting a business

I don’t know if this was always a myth, but at least nowdays in many european countries it’s just as easy. The only reason why europeans don’t is because we don’t need to thanks to the welfare.

>>62261523
Unless you really luck out, all the extra income goes towards things europeans get for free.

62261563
>>62261551
really doubt it. Engineers here make 2-3k /month they can get 150k in the us. Some people get much more. That's commie cope

62261586
>>62261563
In my country they make 3k at start and over 4k easily, and of course they can get bigger salaries too. It’s a small number of engineers who get 150k in US. Overall there really isn’t an improvement in life quality, and you would have to leave all your friends and family.

62261587
>>62261414
Happy birthday anon. The world is unfair and many who have been successful are finding themselves in dire straits due to how awful everything is.
I think there is always the chance for things to the upside and have faith in you. Good luck.

62262137
At least you tried.

62262164
>>62261490
>>62261501
Cmon now, don't encourage suicide Baldie schizo, I'm the sick-anon, I'm gonna be honest I was not in a good way and you saying that kinda shit wasn't helping, luckily my good friend and beloved companion in this bullshit we call life was able to lift my spirits.
But cmon now even if OP is a faggot, it's easier to be nice than it is to be mean because anger reflects back onto you.
Even you were worried I killed myself when I was trying to sell my guns, yes I do own guns so watch who you say that kinda shit to brother.
I say this for your own sake, while I slept you worried even if it wasn't your focus you did worry.
It was me samefagging as you btw.
I was gonna crank it to 11 until everyone hated you but I backed off, I've ran many people off this board before by copying their style/namefag and annoying people on purpose.

62262734
>>62261414
Try and relax and enjoy your bday
I can relate but not that bad. Seems u had it rough. Hard times pass.
Do something you wanna do today

62262752
>>62262164
im sorry but freedom of speech always come first. If you're going to blow your brain because of a 4 chan anon comment you were likely to do it anyway without it. this is a peculiar joy to saying what the fuck you want to say.

also you can't run me out of this board, we are probably having convo in other thread without even realizing it, since we are max 10 to 20 regular posters here

62262796
>>62261586
It's only low iq third worlders who think American salary is all that matters. EU quality of life is so much better

62262803
>>62261414
30y old.
You are baby.
Keep calm, enjoy your life

62262809
>>62262752
>I'm le victim
No just stop being an asshat. You're free to say what you want and we can call you douchebag with our own freedom of speech.

62262866
>>62262164
this is one of the saddest things I've ever read. When did you last have sex you fucking tranny freak

62262905
>>62261414
go work in a warehouse

62262915
>>62261414
Check out buildnow. 6 to 8 week program and you'll have a borderline guaranteed 80k a year data center job. Its not over fren.

62262933
>>62261512
bro im literal fucking developer, and I would get paid 200k+ in the US instead of fucking 80k euros in stupid europe.
plus taxes rape me and we don't have AC here, stfu. i hate you faggots
>>62261414
happy nigger day OP, try to milk the states, the money printer is going to ran out and we'll be full serfs forever. better get grinding, pray to God for strength and you'll be okay. Literally you cannot be poor if you're smart, but yes wagecucking sucks

62253216
>>62252856
Crazy I'm from lebanon too and someone didn't send me any btc because I'm not a serial begger, I too could use a very much needed 2k right now crazy!

62253224
>>62252856
This guys an israeli scammer btw he just makes scams out of any trend clearly he struck it big in 2020 as he said someone sent him multiple bitcoins and he's still here pretending.
He know he isn't from Lebanon but his location came up Florida multiple times when the sharty hack was released he was a trial janitor here.

62253228
>>62252856
He's also pretending his sister killed herself and asking for money for it in XMR threads.
Last it was his dad that died but only after he got 8k for it.
Fucker has probably got 7 figures

62254644
>>62253228
8K?
nigger I would fucking beat you up just because you're that spiteful
I never claimed I had a sister
>>62253216
iza 3anjad e7ki 3arabe yalla
>>62253224
>He know he isn't from Lebanon but his location came up Florida multiple times when the sharty hack was released he was a trial janitor here.
kek
I posted proof so many times
seethe more jew

62254676
>>62253224
>multiple bitcoins
>multiple bitcoins
>multiple bitcoins
how dense are you
I even showed the screencap it was like 300$ total

62255610
>>62254676
>>62254644
Ah look the scammers still here even after mass reporting my thread lol, schizo scammer melty schizo melty lol fucking faggot I got your number, next your identity.
I got some faggots from /g/ working on it.
You just thought you were immune to exposure.
You're dead wrong and this time I want to see who you are.

62256655
>>62255610
Moron

62258565
>>62253066

62261255
>>62254644
Stay away Lebanon boy

62261309
>>62219942

62261638
>>62261309

62261762

item.title: I hold thousands of LINK and regularly spread Chainlink FUD — 26 replies — 6.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I hold thousands of LINK and regularly spread Chainlink FUD

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-22 13:58 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62257369
>>62257367
you should have sold 5 years ago, hell I sold in 2022 (at $13) and got called retarded by the cult but it turned out to be a fantastic decision.

62257370
>>62257369
>you should have sold 5 years ago
>he doesn't know

62257380
>>62257370
you're right it was 6 years ago to be exact, the peak was August 2020. I still remember those days of pure euphoria mooning against the entire market.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62257498
>>62257380
>he still doesn't know

62257507
>>62257367
don't we all
still buying to this day

62257511
>>62257369
>>62257370
>>62257380
>>62257498
>>62257507

KAAABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOMMMMM!!!!!!!!

62258063
>>62257498
doesnt know what, stinkie? are you implying link is gonna le mooooon to $1000 soon?
cuz if so, eat shit
eat literal excrement

62258569
>>62257367
One day you'll wake up and the realization of all that wasted time and cost opportunity spent on post ironic memes will hit you all at once and you'll blow your brains out. Along with every other bagholder who killed themselves as well

62258599
>>62257367
I have 60k Link staked in the community pool and 23k not staked. I'll be staking those when the pool expands. Also I have 629K SXT staked and 139K FHE staked, that I got for nothing from Build rewards.
I partly (though by no means fully) have fudders to thank for my diamond hands. It was abundantly clear from their obsession that Link was the one. And here we are. Nearly there.

62259006
>>62258599
83k LINK is a fat stack. Why did you not sell at $50? Could have bought literally any blue chip stock and have $10M+ by now. Anyway, I'm jealous. I have worked up to just over 60k now, though I had way less back then or I definitely would have sold.

62259041
>>62259006
Why did (You) not sell at $50?
And then bought vaccine stocks and sold the top?
And then bought oil futures and sold the top?
And then bought NVDA and sold the top?
Or even better, SanDisk?

62259090
>>62258599
>Nearly there.
nearly at the poorhouse, yes stinkie
the dump to zero is gonna be so sweet

62259157
>>62259041
No no no linky, you don't get to use those standards.

Why didn't YOU use you're foresight to perfectly buy the bottom and perfectly sell the top

62259345
>>62259006
>Why did you not sell at $50?
Because the pool is closed.

62260007
>>62259157
Just know the future bro just buy every bottom sell every top Bro its simple

62260100
>>62259345
because the cope is cope
fixed that for u, stankie

62260833
I have a large stack of Link and wont be selling at $50 the next time, either. Imagine kek

62260933
>>62260833
but if you did you would be rich right now
you lost the game of life, stinkbat
it's ogre

62261383
>>62260933
>you would be rich right now
and i'm not? trouble with small minded ppl is you're always unable to think bigger than your own world

62261432
>>62261383
You’re a cuckold who married his 2017 shitcoin bags. Have fun missing another bullrun in 2028

62261460
>>62257367
Me too. And I used to harass Sergey Nazarov on Twitter almost daily from 2019 to 2022.

62261485
>>62261432
Wow. This guy has wasted a good portion of his life sitting here typing this shit.
Anyway, I'm out. Was nice to revisit after an abscence, but I now remember why I left.

62261589
>>62257367
I have only a ~5k stack (staked), bought most of it 2018 when I was still in school and Link 20 cents. Then went to university and only last year started my carreer and getting a regular wage.
Thanks for keeping the price down so I can finally accumulate more.

62261766
>>62257367
stop any time you want anon, chainlink is a good project and company

62261873
>>62258063
Why can't link moon to 1k and beyond?

62262205
>>62257367
No you dont and you have no argument for those that do fud which is why you post this to hope people will believe you. Lol Insane levels of being down bad.
